Central Europe Travel Guide: Prague & Vienna

This guide covers recommended restaurants, hotels, and highlights for Prague and Vienna.

Vienna (Wien)

Austria's capital blends imperial grandeur with a vibrant contemporary scene.

Restaurants

  • Figlmüller Bäckerstraße — Famous for its enormous Wiener Schnitzel. Price range: €€€. Cuisine: Austrian.
  • Zum Wohl — Excellent Austrian wine bar with cold cuts and cheese. Price range: €€. Cuisine: Austrian/Wine.

Hotels

  • Hotel Sacher Wien — Iconic luxury hotel, home of the original Sachertorte. Price: €€€€. Parking: available.

Highlights

  • Schönbrunn Palace — UNESCO World Heritage baroque palace with formal gardens.
  • Belvedere Museum — Home to Klimt's "The Kiss".
  • St. Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)

Milan (Milano)

Italy's fashion and finance capital, also home to world-class art and cuisine.

Restaurants

  • Trattoria Milanese — Classic Milanese cuisine since 1933, try the risotto alla Milanese. Price: €€€. Cuisine: Italian/Milanese.
  • Luini — Famous street-food spot for panzerotti (fried dough pockets). Price: €. Cuisine: Street food.

Accommodation

  • Airbnb Navigli District — Stylish apartment in the trendy canal district. Price: €€. Area: Navigli.

Sights

  • The Last Supper (Cenacolo Vinciano) — Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece, book well in advance.
  • Duomo di Milano — Gothic cathedral with rooftop terrace views.
